happens very often it just drives you crazy is let's say you get through the gatekeeper the first time and you have this awesome conversation with the decision maker and you hit it off you love your product get the qualified guy he's totally qualified you send the guys some information you schedule a call back for the next for five days later and now you get screened out you can't get through the gatekeeper a second time that happens all the fucking time so what do you do then let's talk about that okay the most effective strategy I've ever used for this and it worked so well guys it was a investment right it was called how to make money in common stocks we start off then I was I would send them a copy of personal investor magazine and I went to a magazine for a while but I would send and if you're trying to close someone online you're gonna have to write the price down so they have to buy it if you're looking to create leads online no you wouldn't have to say the price you could just talk about the value remember this okay the big mistake people make when they sell off it is they try to um they try to equate the service they give or the time spent giving that service to some value like that how like let's say let's say I'm a consultant and I spend one hour working on your company how much is that hour worth I mean how much is my time worth that's one way to look at it or how much money can you make by taking my advice let's say I spend one hour and I give you a distinction a strategy it allows you to make an extra five million a year then what was that hour worth it's worth probably a million bucks so you can't trade time for money you're never gonna do very well in this world selling and pricing if you try to sell your product based on just like it's priced for time it's about how much money you're saving what the outcome you're giving I wouldn't post it online to price unless you're trying to close them online okay the next question comes in from what's not millions and what kind of expectations and discipline is required listen Nick you know the key I mean I just spoke at the door-to-door conference recently it was great a great group of people here and the key to building effective teams is really at that very very close to supervised management in other words you want to have people that you want you don't want to let your salesman go run wild in the field you know I've seen people that go as far as where like in the solar industry we're at a company I train where they actually had drivers in vans taking sales people to certain neighborhoods and they have door knockers and they were really supervising at a not level and they let the cream rise to the top so they have door knockers and then those people would set up appointments for the closures and so on right and you know how do you do anything it's very simple you know you take you let you have when you're trying to build a millionaires you know group you gotta do it based on egalitarian means you know it's gotta be a meritocracy the cream rises to the top the people that perform get more support they get people working for them and slowly but sure you build this infrastructure up where these people become magicians and that's a key now one important thing here when the one mistake I see people make a lot when they're trying to build the sales force is they take the top sales person and they move them into becoming a sales manager and what typically happens is a very it's a very dicey business because a salesman makes the most money when they sell and when you turn it into a manager they typically stop selling and they make a lot less so be very careful about on when you promote sales people don't necessarily promote them into becoming managers you might promote them by just giving them more people to work for them to let them do the more mundane tests like cold corn regeneration paperwork so you support your top sales people by allowing them to leverage off your infrastructure all they have to do is sell don't necessarily promote them sideways into other jobs managing because that's not going to be in your best interest most of the time and just remember supervise your people closely break them up into teams you want to if you want to build million dollar guys you have us being very close support don't let them all run wild in the field it's very difficult to run a large door-to-door organization without close supervision and also give meetings every day every day they have to have a meeting in the morning right put them on the day they put them every person so she's be on the daily motivations perfectly designed for that they got every day they got to be anchored they should do all beyond the daily motivation with me and then that so okay so i'll answer your question and i also would suggest that you you know go into the search bar and punch an action threshold and you see a lot of different trainings i've had some really really great ones i'm explaining like 20 different ways and i'll continue explaining it 20 more different ways because each time i explain it's a little bit different and maybe hits a little button right and the stuff never gets old it's you need to always be reinforced in short an action threshold represents the sum of someone's buying beliefs the beliefs they have about buying about making decisions about trusting people trusting salespeople and it's essentially how they view the world all of those belief systems wrap up into one cohesive package that results in them landing at a certain spot in terms of how easy are they to sell to in other words let's be logical if someone has a set of beliefs that say my best decisions are made slowly i shouldn't trust salespeople they mostly out to rip me off i think i should always check with other people before i make any decision if those are like their beliefs they have that's going to be someone that's very very tough to sell to most companies are out to get me most of the time people give me shitty customer service you know those are all beliefs they're going to lead someone to make very slow decisions and also have to be at a level of absolute certainty on all three tens before they have any shot of buying so in other words i always say i use the example my father and me right i'm a very easy person to sell to i have a very low action threshold meaning that the level of certain the collective level of certainty for the three tens so how much i have to love a product how much i have to trust the salesperson present in the product how much of the trust to get with the company standing behind that product those three things well for me i don't have to be at a 10 10 10 if i'm no at an eight i'm really it seems like a good product seems salesman seems like a nice guy company seems okay i'm like a seven eight eight i might buy especially if there's a little bit of pain i'm feeling or it's not that expensive in other words the energy in is not that high money right conversely someone like my father who's very very tough to sell to is very high action threshold you know he's got to be at a 10 10 10 and damn sure of it you know why because he has beliefs that say i don't trust salespeople i gotta think things over i gotta do my research so it's like my best decision to make very very slowly you get it now those now it's pretty logical right i don't need to take that further right but now let's talk about what that really translates into so someone with those sets of beliefs about the slow decision making not trusting they make a decision in one way and people like me you're very trusting and basically you don't have to you know yeah my best to usually make quickly and you know i that's called low axe right i my decision is made another way let me explain the difference when i'm about to make a decision i run two movies parallel movies i always say what's my upside if if this product turns out to be as good as the salesman says what's my life going to look like after i buy what's my upside how's it going to improve my life and i'll always also run the downside and if the sales is a lying feeding piece of shit and he rips me off how much is it going to negatively impact my life the instant i'm presented with a buying up they say you know in the s for the order my brain and instantly projects forward and i analyze those things that happen so fast it's like a tent a million a millionth of a say that's how fast it happens on the recorder for a second right now when i do that i run both of those movies i run the the upside movie and i run the downside movie when my father makes a decision he does the same thing he runs an upside movie and he runs a downside movie the difference is is that my father will run a really long-winded very clear very colorful downside movie he actually thinks out the negative because all he projects it out and before he's done his mind his brain has him making the decision if he does this he'll be in the worst shape ever it'll destroy his life he'll be embarrassed he'll feel terrible his wife will hate him he'll take up room in his closet he'll probably steal his credit confirmation he'll don't return it he'll be embarrassed in front of his neighbors his life's going to be destroyed will make his problem worse and before he's done this decision is like become the worst fucking decision he ever made in his life and then when it comes to running the positive movie meaning that if he bought it and it worked would it improve his life he's like eh maybe he runs it but not that he's the blunts the movie so he runs an exaggerated bad movie and a very blunt good movie he's like eh maybe i see i'm uh what i don't work me on the other end i do the opposite i run a really really long positive movie and i run a very short negative movie so obviously if if someone's running a really long toxic negative movie and they get to buy no they're probably not going to buy and if someone does the opposite with a positive movie no negative they're probably going to buy so that's really what's going on with someone's action threshold that's what's going on clinically so to speak right the way we are able to then circumvent that is by using the power of language we hijack their strategy so we know they're running these two movies everybody is so rather than letting someone like my father run this ridiculous very long-winded movie and very short and positive one i take control of his movie by after i run my next loop
